'''''The Wookiee Storybook''''' is a [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] picture book written by [[Eleanor Ehrhardt]] and illustrated by [[Patricia Wynne]]. It follows the young [[Wookiee]] [[Lumpawaroo/Legends|Lumpy]] as he travels to the [[Shadowlands/Legends|Nother World]] to collect [[Wasaka berry/Legends|wasaka berries]] for [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca's]] two-hundredth [[Birthday/Legends|birthday]]. The book was published by [[Random House]] on [[September 12]], [[1979]] and was one of the few works licensed by the short-lived [[Lucasfilm]] subsidiary [[Black Falcon]]

==Publisher's summary==
''Lumpy's descent to the nother world ''[''sic'']'' on his jungle planet to hunt for special berries for his father's homecoming becomes a scary adventure.''

==Plot summary==
On the jungle planet [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], the young [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] Lumpawarrump (Lumpy) and his mother, [[Mallatobuck/Legends|Mallatobuck]] (Malla), are preparing a surprise party for [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], Lumpy's father and Malla's [[Marriage/Legends|husband]]. It is Chewbacca's two-hundredth [[Birthday/Legends|birthday]], and he is returning to Kashyyyk after traveling with the [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] for three [[Standard year/Legends|years]]. Malla wants to make [[wasaka-berry pudding]], which Chewbacca loves, but she does not have enough [[Wasaka berry/Legends|wasaka berries]]. Lumpy asks if he can go get the berries, which are located in the dangerous "Nother World." Malla does not want Lumpy to get hurt, so she refuses, upsetting Lumpy. Lumpy's grandfather, [[Attichitcuk/Legends|Attichitcuk]] (Itchy) decides to cheer up the young Wookiee by telling him a story of Chewbacca's heroism.

[[File:Wookstoryscan.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Lumpy journeys into the Nother World.]]
Itchy tells Lumpy that long ago, the Wookiees came to Kashyyyk from another [[Unidentified planet (Wookiees)|planet]]. However, one [[Standard day/Legends|day]], [[Slaver/Legends|slavers]] came to Kashyyyk and captured several Wookiees, including Chewbacca, and took them to the slavers' planet. When Solo visited the planet, the pilot helped the Wookiees free themselves from the slavers. From then on, Chewbacca had the honor of being Solo's copilot. Itchy concludes his story and describes what the upcoming bicentennial party will be like.

Lumpy becomes inspired by Itchy's story and decides to go get the wasaka berries, despite Malla's warning. Lumpy begins to climb down into the Nother World, but he slips and plummets into a [[Swamp/Legends|swamp]]. The [[Species/Legends|beasts]] in the Nother World chase Lumpy until he dives into a log to hide. Meanwhile, Malla realizes that Lumpy has disappeared, so she sends [[thought wave]]s to Chewbacca to come quickly. Chewbacca receives the message and flies an [[explorer craft]] away from the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] to save Lumpy. Chewbacca finds Lumpy and returns with him to the house. Chewbacca tells Lumpy that he is a hero for never giving up while trapped in the Nother World. After arriving back home, Chewbacca is surprised by the birthday party, and hundreds of Wookiees celebrate.

==Continuity==
{{Quote|When I set out to recast and redeem the Lumpy story in ''The Black Fleet Crisis'', I was drawing on a copy of the ''Star Wars Storybook'' which Lucasfilm loaned to me after turning down my proposal to give Chewbacca a polygamous family—I was obliged to work with the family Chewbacca already had.|Michael P. Kube-McDowell|<ref name="Lucasfan" />}}
''The Wookiee Storybook'' features Chewbacca's family and home, which first appeared in the [[1978]] television movie ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]''.<ref name="Year by Year" />

The [[1996]] novel ''[[Tyrant's Test]]'' references the events of ''The Wookiee Storybook'', revealing that Lumpy's misadventure had grown among his friends and family with each retelling. According to ''Tyrant's Test'' author [[Michael P. Kube-McDowell]], his proposal to give Chewbacca a polygamous family in ''Tyrant's Test'' was turned down by [[Lucasfilm]], who cited ''The Wookiee Storybook'' and loaned him a copy.<ref name="Lucasfan">{{WebCite|url=https://www.lucasfan.com/swtv/index3.html|text=Star Wars Holiday Special Opinions|work=[https://www.lucasfan.com LucasFan.com]|archivedate=20190627013454}}</ref>

The events of ''The Wookiee Storybook'' are also referenced in the [[2018]] book ''[[Solo: A Star Wars Story: Tales from Vandor]]''.<ref name="Tales from Vandor">''[[Solo: A Star Wars Story: Tales from Vandor]]''</ref>
